title: lgtm canonical rule model
description: Agent-neutral engineering rule. One structured object per rule; the operational source of truth compiled into the lgtm binary.

id.description: Stable, immutable rule identifier. Kebab-case; never reused for a different rule.

description.description: What the rule requires and why, in plain language.

mechanism.description: Honest enforcement capability, independent of the rule's task instruction.

confidence.description: Confidence that the declared mechanism proves the rule for its scope.

examples.description: Short good/bad examples shown when the rule is activated.

enforcement_stage.description: Lifecycle stage at which the capability runs.

language_implementations.description: Optional language-specific checks that narrow the base capability.

severity.description: How a violation is reported by the enforcement runtime.

level.description: Enforcement strength. must = blocks completion; should = expected by default; review = triggers inspection.

category.description: Rule category derived from the coding-standards taxonomy.

applies_to.description: Scope filter: which languages, domains, and file patterns the rule can apply to.

languages.description: Languages the rule can apply to. An empty array means the rule applies to all languages; a non-empty array restricts it to the listed languages.

domains.description: Domains the rule can apply to. An empty array means the rule applies to all domains; a non-empty array restricts it to the listed domains.

file_patterns.description: File glob patterns the rule can apply to. An empty array means the rule applies to all files; a non-empty array restricts it to files matching the listed patterns.

activation.description: Deterministic signals that switch the rule on for a task.

change_types.description: Change kinds that switch the rule on. An empty array means the rule activates for any change kind; a non-empty array restricts activation to the listed change kinds.

signals.description: Deterministic signals that switch the rule on. An empty array means no signal is required to activate; a non-empty array restricts activation to tasks carrying one of the listed signals.

instruction.description: Compact directive injected into the agent context when the rule activates.

overridable.description: Whether repo-local config may downgrade or disable the rule. Security-critical rules are false.

evidence.description: Evidence artifacts a task must produce for this rule.

references.description: Source anchors in the human standards this rule derives from.
